commonIssues:
The 7/12 extract often contains outdated owner names, missing inheritance records, or incorrect land area details, making it difficult to verify clear title.

How to fix:

  • Apply online for correction via Mahabhulekh or E-Hakk portal, or visit Talathi. Mahabhumi Portal

Older manual records (pre-digitization) often conflict with new digital entries, causing confusion regarding survey numbers (Gat Number) or property boundaries.

How to fix:

  • Visit the Sub-Divisional Officer (SDO) for manual verification and record rectification.

Many buyers fail to check if the previous owner has updated the mutation entry, meaning the property is still recorded in the seller's name despite a sale deed.

How to fix:

  • Ensure the seller updates their name on the 7/12 extract before purchase. Check status on Mahabhumi portal.

Underestimating the market value based on outdated Ready Reckoner (RR) rates leads to deficits in stamp duty calculation.

How to fix:

  • Verify current RR rates on the IGR Maharashtra portal before calculating stamp duty. IGR Maharashtra

Non-Marathi speakers often struggle with local land revenue terms on 7/12 extracts, leading to errors in document submission.

How to fix:

  • Consult a local legal expert or use the official glossary on the IGR website.

Failing to check the EC leaves buyers unaware of potential legal disputes or bank loans secured against the property.

How to fix:

  • Download EC online from IGR Maharashtra to check past 30 years of transactions.

Even after registration, updating the official land record to reflect the new owner (mutation/Ferfar entry) can take months at the Talathi office.

How to fix:

  • Track application on Aaple Sarkar or E-Hakk. File grievance if delayed. Aaple Sarkar

If the chain of documents is incomplete or if legal heirs challenge the transaction, the mutation request may be rejected.

How to fix:

  • Provide a complete chain of deeds and family tree (Vanshaval) if challenged.

In urban areas, the property card may not reflect new subdivisions or building redevelopments promptly, leading to legal disputes.

How to fix:

  • Apply to the City Survey Officer (CTSO) with approved building plans for updating property cards.

As of 2026, errors in stamp duty payment are penalized heavily, with penalties increasing significantly.

How to fix:

  • Pay deficit stamp duty via GRAS (Government Receipt Accounting System). GRAS Portal

In co-operative housing societies, failure to receive the No Objection Certificate (NOC) or proper society transfer documents leads to issues.

How to fix:

  • Obtain NOC and share certificate transfer from the society secretary before registering the deed.
